  "text": "func TestCredentialRevocationJob_Run(t *testing.T) {
	// t.Parallel() - this was causing test failures, investigate before un-commenting
n1: 	ctx := context.Background()
n2: 	assert, require := assert.New(t), require.New(t)

n3: 	v := NewTestVaultServer(t, WithDockerNetwork(true))
n4: 	testDb := v.MountDatabase(t)

n5: 	conn, _ := db.TestSetup(t, "postgres")
n6: 	rw := db.New(conn)
n7: 	wrapper := db.TestWrapper(t)
n8: 	org, prj := iam.TestScopes(t, iam.TestRepo(t, conn, wrapper))
n9: 	kmsCache := kms.TestKms(t, conn, wrapper)
n10: 	sche := scheduler.TestScheduler(t, conn, wrapper)
n11: 	repo, err := NewRepository(ctx, rw, rw, kmsCache, sche)
n12: 	require.NoError(err)

n13: 	_, token := v.CreateToken(t, WithPolicies([]string{"default", "boundary-controller", "database"}))
n14: 	credStoreIn, err := NewCredentialStore(prj.GetPublicId(), v.Addr, []byte(token))
n15: 	require.NoError(err)
n16: 	j, err := newTokenRenewalJob(ctx, rw, rw, kmsCache)
n17: 	require.NoError(err)
n18: 	err = sche.RegisterJob(ctx, j)
n19: 	require.NoError(err)
n20: 	cs, err := repo.CreateCredentialStore(ctx, credStoreIn)
n21: 	require.NoError(err)

n22: 	libPath := path.Join("database", "creds", "opened")
n23: 	libIn, err := NewCredentialLibrary(cs.GetPublicId(), libPath)
n24: 	require.NoError(err)
n25: 	cl, err := repo.CreateCredentialLibrary(ctx, prj.GetPublicId(), libIn)
n26: 	require.NoError(err)

n27: 	at := authtoken.TestAuthToken(t, conn, kmsCache, org.GetPublicId())
n28: 	uId := at.GetIamUserId()
n29: 	hc := static.TestCatalogs(t, conn, prj.GetPublicId(), 1)[0]
n30: 	hs := static.TestSets(t, conn, hc.GetPublicId(), 1)[0]
n31: 	h := static.TestHosts(t, conn, hc.GetPublicId(), 1)[0]
n32: 	static.TestSetMembers(t, conn, hs.GetPublicId(), []*static.Host{h})
n33: 	tar := tcp.TestTarget(ctx, t, conn, prj.GetPublicId(), "test", target.WithHostSources([]string{hs.GetPublicId()}))
n34: 	sess := session.TestSession(t, conn, wrapper, session.ComposedOf{
		UserId:      uId,
		HostId:      h.GetPublicId(),
		TargetId:    tar.GetPublicId(),
		HostSetId:   hs.GetPublicId(),
		AuthTokenId: at.GetPublicId(),
		ProjectId:   prj.GetPublicId(),
		Endpoint:    "tcp://127.0.0.1:22",
	})

n35: 	repoToken := allocToken()
n36: 	require.NoError(rw.LookupWhere(ctx, &repoToken, "token_hmac = ?", []any{cs.outputToken.TokenHmac}))

n37: 	r, err := newCredentialRevocationJob(ctx, rw, rw, kmsCache)
n38: 	require.NoError(err)

n39: 	err = r.Run(ctx)
n40: 	require.NoError(err)
	// No credentials should have been revoked
n41: 	assert.Equal(0, r.numCreds)

n42: 	secret1, _ := testVaultCred(t, conn, v, cl, sess, repoToken, ActiveCredential, 5*time.Minute)
n43: 	revokeSecret, revokeCred := testVaultCred(t, conn, v, cl, sess, repoToken, RevokeCredential, 5*time.Minute)
n44: 	secret2, _ := testVaultCred(t, conn, v, cl, sess, repoToken, RevokedCredential, 5*time.Minute)
n45: 	secret3, _ := testVaultCred(t, conn, v, cl, sess, repoToken, ExpiredCredential, 5*time.Minute)

	// Verify the revokeCred has a status of revoke
n46: 	lookupCred := allocCredential()
n47: 	lookupCred.PublicId = revokeCred.PublicId
n48: 	require.NoError(rw.LookupById(ctx, lookupCred))
n49: 	assert.Equal(string(RevokeCredential), lookupCred.Status)

	// Verify revokeCred is valid in testDb
n50: 	assert.NoError(testDb.ValidateCredential(t, revokeSecret))

n51: 	err = r.Run(ctx)
n52: 	require.NoError(err)
	// The revoke credential should have been revoked
n53: 	assert.Equal(1, r.numCreds)

	// revokeCred should now have a status of revoked
n54: 	lookupCred = allocCredential()
n55: 	lookupCred.PublicId = revokeCred.PublicId
n56: 	require.NoError(rw.LookupById(ctx, lookupCred))
n57: 	assert.Equal(string(RevokedCredential), lookupCred.Status)

	// revokeCred should no longer be valid in test database
n58: 	assert.Error(testDb.ValidateCredential(t, revokeSecret))

	// Other creds should still be valid in test database
n59: 	assert.NoError(testDb.ValidateCredential(t, secret1))
n60: 	assert.NoError(testDb.ValidateCredential(t, secret2))
n61: 	assert.NoError(testDb.ValidateCredential(t, secret3))
}
"
